what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment ledger entry: YAML frontmatter declaring an image attachment (att-78dca726) in the design-source channel that points to the image resource image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-11 and records when it was added.
observations: YAML frontmatter only; no body content
target uses an image:// URI scheme
added timestamp is precise to microseconds with timezone
id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields are present
standouts: Typed as an images attachment in the design-source channel
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter in a .md file
Follows a fixed metadata schema: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added
Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with explicit UTC offset
References an external asset by URI rather than embedding it
concerns: No body or caption describing the image
Target URI is opaque and not verifiable from this file alone
No author or provenance field beyond the timestamp
notes: A bare attachment metadata stub linking the design-source channel to an image asset for a recent pitch.
questions: What pitch or message does p-11 correspond to?
Who added this attachment and why?
Is there a caption, thumbnail, or alternate format?
apparent_themes: Separation of metadata from media payload
intention_signals: To record that an image asset was attached to the design-source channel in association with a recent pitch (p-11).
novelty: Uses an image:// URI target in an otherwise conventional YAML ledger entry.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed pointer that links a channel memory entry to an external image asset without embedding the asset itself.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record (att-78dca726) in the design-source channel of type images pointing to image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-11, added 2026-06-22T11:31:02Z.
intention_for_embedding: To log an image attachment associated with a recent pitch in the design-source channel.
